steps.title: Identify the cost driver
steps.content: The passage says HILDA is expensive because longitudinal studies require years of data collection. The fitness study is contrasted as relatively straightforward, so the blank must explain why it avoids that same time-based cost.

steps.title: Reject unrelated contrasts
steps.content: A reverses the logic by saying longitudinal methods suit the fitness study. B and C focus on HILDA details that do not explain why the fitness study would need less money. The remaining choice says the fitness study can avoid years of data collection. The answer is D.
